title: Hide Groups (even if they have content) from the kanban view
excerpt: I’ve had to split work between two boards because I can’t choose which groups to show in the kanban board. I now have to check and report on two separate boards.

posts.author: steven.glickman (Steven Glickman)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve had to split work between two boards because I can’t choose which groups to show in the kanban board. I now have to check and report on two separate boards.

posts.author: mviquez (Michael Viquez)
posts.cookedHtml:
That you be a good feature. Mabybe this can work?

If there are only certain status that you will view in the kanban board, and you do not to toggle them, create a secondary status column. Then create an automation that "when status A changes, then change status B to new status. One make the automations for the status that you want to change.

posts.author: steven.glickman (Steven Glickman)
posts.cookedHtml: That doesn’t salve the problem of having columns for status options that I don’t want to show on the board. There are a total of 7 status options. Only 4 of them impact my engineers. I only want to show those 4 columns. This is basic stuff JIRA has been doing for more than a decade.

posts.author: mviquez (Michael Viquez)
posts.cookedHtml: ALright. I thought I understood. If you do the secondary status (with only 4 statuses) then you could change the Kanban Column to the secondary, and only 4 will should.

posts.author: TanyaB (Tanya B)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would really like to see this. I have about 8 status filters currently, 2 of them being “Complete” and “N/A”. When I am in a Kanban, I don’t want to see 100 items in the N/A and the Completed status cards. This makes lots of cards which makes it hard to easily see. I just want to be able to see the cards for a couple of the status.
I know some have said to use a secondary status however there will always be something eg a blank field, taking up space on the cards

posts.author: LouiseD27 (Louise Dinkelmann)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there.

Don’t know if you found the solution in the mean time, but I also struggled a bit with this one. It is actually easy - just click on the more actions … and hide the statuses that you don’t want to see on your view.
